name: THE POCKET HOTEL Kyoto-Karasumagojo
detail: This has got to be the best budget hotel I've ever been to! I think their kiosk for check-in and check-out is nice and convenient to use. The room size of the single room was perfect and private. It's soundproof so I can sleep soundly or talk freely on the phone. I even get a really nice view of the city from my 9th floor room. The bed was very comfortable and easy to assemble for sleeping. I didn't have to fuss around to put in the sheets. The shared bathroom and shower was so clean and well maintained. I also love how there's cups at the side so I can drink water. The shower stall is actually bigger than I expected which is great. Staff are very helpful and nice. I had some mishaps but they gladly helped me each time. Overall, the whole hotel is very pristine, and even for a low price, I got such a good experience with their service. You can tell that this hotel paid attention to every single detail their target market needs. Solid 10/10 for me.

name: Sotetsu Fresa Inn Kyoto-Shijokarasuma
detail: We stayed here for 6 nights which is a relatively long time in a hotel so we got to really experience the service. It was great. The room we had was quite large compared to most we’ve experienced in Japan (we had a large twin room). The beds were really comfortable and had that luxury feel that often comes with good quality bedding and great pillows. The room was quiet (but we haven’t come across a noisy room yet in Japan - they don’t often use car horns thankfully) and our sleep was never disturbed. We and breakfast at the hotel several times - it was Japanese style but with enough variety to suit most people. One of the best things about the hotel was it’s lcoation. There are two of them in Kyoto. This is the one closest to Nishiki Markets. We were literally a block away from the main shopping area, and short walk to many attractions. Subways and the main lines were all within a minute or two also. Highly recommend this hotel.

name: Sakura Terrace The Gallery -Adult Only-
detail: As soon as we arrived we knew we were going to have a faboulous stay Recepcion hall is an open courtyard where a nice open fireplace warms you up as soon as you arrive There is also a great bar (with an other fireplace) My boyfriend is a smoker and as in Japan smoking areas are really hard to find he really enjoyed this one as it was in a great space. After our welcome drink (which was amazing!!) we got few information about the hotel and we have been really pleased discovering the Onsen was a free in house guest facility The Onsen was great we put on our super comfy kimono and enjoyed the hot water tub. Staff was great and really helpful, they even helped us book a great traditional dinner in a restaurant. We didn't really want to leave! I highly recommend this hotel in Kyoto

name: The Millennials Kyoto
detail: This is the only capsule hotel in Kyoto that has a mixed floor, meaning a floor for both guy & girl which is convenient for a couple like us travelling as we don't want to be on different floor for the night. The room is just a bed with all the remote & key being the Ipod that was passed to you during check in. The troublesome thing is to make sure the ipod is fully charged at all time if not, it will affect on how you can control the light, air & stuffs. There is a place for everyone to mingle & eat. Cups & plates are also provided but do remember to do your own washing once you are done.

name: Sotetsu Fresa Inn Kyoto-Hachijoguchi
detail: This hotel was a jem, just a 5-7 mins walk to the main Kyoto station. The hotel feels very very new and rooms were well cleaned. However, we had the hotel lobby staff unable to converse in English thus we had a hard time trying to communicate with them. Although there was a language barrier, the staff had remained professional and patient while I tried to articulate my needs. At this moment, Sotetsu Fresa Inn Kyoto is the number one of my list of hotels in Kyoto.

name: APA Hotel Kyoto-Ekimae
detail: The APA Hotel in Kyoto was a good hotel in a great location. The CUBE is right next door for dining and shopping, and your Shinkansen is right there for departure as well. There's a Bic Camera adjacent, if you need electronics. A coin laundry is 15 minutes walk east, by the river. It is entirely possible to walk to Chion-in and Kiyomizu-dera from the hotel - I did it. It'll make you feel like a pilgrim. (Takes about 45 minutes to get there) Really, a great place to stay. It's comparable to the Granvia, but - not being in the station itself - much more affordable.

name: Livemax Hostels Kyoto Ekimae
detail: I stayed in LiveMax and it was an amazing experience!

name: ibis Styles Kyoto Shijo
detail: very impressed! although room was small which most Japan hotels are, it was really clean & comfortable. almost all toiletries needed are being provided including pyjamas as well. don't waste your luggage space, they even provide bath salts, body scrub towels, body lotion, shaving cream & after shave lotion! location was perfect for us, karasuma station is just a 5mins walk, bus stops located just outside & directly opposite hotel, convenient store is just across the street, 5 mins walk to the sheltered shopping streets at shijo karasuma, 10-15 mins walk to nishiki market/teramachi, another 5 mins to pontocho, another 5 mins to shijo gion area. many Kyoto city bus available for all famous attractions, we even manage to get a bus here to go to Ohara. Our one & only complaint was that the pillows provided were really small & flat, tried requesting for additional pillows but as hotel was fully booked, they couldn't provide extras.

name: APA Hotel Kyoto Ekihigashi
detail: Clean place, breakfast is ok, no free parking lots and to park overnight for public place is so expensive. Onsen is free and private. There is washing machine, hotel is clean and new condition. There are 7-11 and Lawson close by. The place is close to Kyoto tower and Isetan and Kyoto station. Totally is ok for spending money here.

name: Ibis Styles Kyoto Station
detail: Having stayed at various hotels in Kyoto before - here's a new addition to the scene which is conveniently located next to Kyoto station. The room was a decent size (by Japanese standards) and we had a nice view over the station, watching the shinkansen come and go. If you arrive by shinkansen from Tokyo, you can see the hotel from the window - so transfer couldn't be easier. Don't worry about breakfast - there are a multitude of great options within a stone's throw!
